hero.title: Real-Time ISRO Satellite Pass Predictor
hero.description: Detect your location, pick an ISRO satellite, and get the next visible pass with weather-based visibility insights.

errors.geolocationNotSupported: Geolocation not supported by this browser.
errors.locationPermissionNeeded: Please allow location access to predict passes.

satellites.subtitle: Explore indigenous satellites powering mapping, communication, and science missions.

map.title: Real-Time Satellite Tracking
map.subtitle: Watch ISRO satellites orbit Earth in real-time with live position updates and orbital paths.
map.noneAboveIndia: None of the monitored satellites are currently above India.
map.noPassesPredicted: No passes predicted in the next 30 minutes

disaster.title: ISRO Assets Protecting India
disaster.subtitle: Swadeshi spacecraft supply constant data for IMD, NDMA, Forest Survey, and farmers. Explore how each hazard taps a unique mix of weather, radar, and communication satellites.

selfReliance.title: Swadeshi self-reliance score
selfReliance.subtitle: Static snapshot showing how much of key services are delivered by Indian space assets. The bars highlight current share handled domestically versus support from foreign constellations.

about.title: ISRO - Building Swadeshi Space Power
about.subtitle: From PSLV to Gaganyaan, Indias space story is one of resilience and frugal innovation. This platform celebrates the satellites and scientists who keep our weather reliable, our farmers empowered, and our defense vigilant.
about.atmanirbharDescription: Indigenous launch vehicles, indigenous avionics, indigenous payloads. The Real-Time ISRO Satellite Pass Predictor inspires students and innovators to build on this legacy with open data and modern geospatial tooling.

cartosat.title: Cartosat Constellation
cartosat.details: High-resolution imaging satellites powering Atmanirbhar mapping, smart city planning, and rapid disaster response.

risat.title: RISAT Radar Eyes
risat.details: All-weather radar satellites that secure our oceans, monitor agriculture, and support strategic reconnaissance.

gsat.title: GSAT Communication Backbone
gsat.details: Geostationary fleet that keeps Bharat connected via broadband, TV, maritime and in-flight communications.

navic.title: NavIC / IRNSS
navic.details: Regional navigation system delivering sub-10m accuracy across land, sea, and air - built entirely in India.

emisat.title: EMISAT Intelligence
emisat.details: Dedicated electromagnetic intelligence platform expanding indigenous space-based surveillance capabilities.

aditya.title: Aditya-L1 Solar Mission
aditya.details: India's first space-based solar observatory studying coronal heating, solar winds, and space weather alerts.

feedback.title: Mission Feedback
feedback.subtitle: Share ideas to improve Swadeshi space awareness tools
feedback.name: Name
feedback.thankYou: Thank you! Your feedback uplinks our mission.

passTable.awaitingPrediction: Awaiting prediction. Choose a satellite and tap Predict to view the next pass.

hub.title: ISRO Knowledge Capsule
hub.subtitle: Dive into curated lessons on indigenous launchers, crew missions, and data standards. Each module is sourced from static JSON, keeping the knowledge base offline friendly.

footer.aboutDescription: Real-time tracking and prediction system for ISRO satellites. Built with modern web technologies for accurate orbital mechanics.
footer.copyright: Swadeshi Space Innovation. All rights reserved.

/learn/orbits.title: Orbit Fundamentals
/learn/orbits.summary: Understand LEO, MEO, GEO, SSO and how TLE + SGP4 power our tracker.

/learn/launchers.title: Launch Vehicles
/learn/launchers.summary: Compare PSLV, GSLV, and LVM3 lift capabilities fueling Swadeshi missions.

/learn/gaganyaan.title: Gaganyaan
/learn/gaganyaan.summary: Crew module, abort tests, and indigenous life-support stack.

/learn/history.title: ISRO History
/learn/history.summary: Chandrayaan to Mangalyaan milestones shaping Atmanirbhar space.

leo.title: Low Earth Orbit (LEO)
leo.description: Altitude 160–2000 km. Hosts Cartosat, RISAT, EOS, enabling high-resolution revisits for land mapping.

meo.title: Medium Earth Orbit (MEO)
meo.description: Altitude 2,000–35,000 km. NavIC constellation flies in inclined geosynchronous slots for regional coverage.

geo.title: Geostationary Orbit (GEO)
geo.description: 36,000 km equatorial orbit matching Earth rotation. INSAT/GSAT deliver uninterrupted telecom and weather feeds.

sso.title: Sun Synchronous Orbit (SSO)
sso.description: Polar orbit with consistent local time passes. Ideal for Earth observation constellations requiring uniform lighting.

tle.title: Two-Line Elements (TLE)
tle.text: Compact NASA/NORAD format describing orbital planes, mean motion, eccentricity, and inclination.

sgp4.title: SGP4 propagation
sgp4.text: Mathematical model that advances TLEs forward in time. Our backend uses tle.js to predict footprints.

project.title: Project link
project.text: Prediction API ingests TLE per satellite, runs SGP4 for pass forecasts, and streams coordinates to the map.

pslv.name: PSLV
pslv.payload: 1.7 t to LEO / 1.1 t to SSO
pslv.description: The workhorse four-stage launcher mixing solid + liquid stages. Ideal for EO missions and commercial rideshares.

gslv-mk-ii.name: GSLV Mk-II
gslv-mk-ii.description: Cryogenic upper stage enables GEO comsat deployments. Powers missions like GISAT and regional communications.

lvm3.name: LVM3
lvm3.payload: 4 t to GTO / 8 t to LEO
lvm3.description: Heavy-lift with twin S200 boosters and CE-20 cryogenic stage. Selected for Gaganyaan crew and deep-space missions.

crew-module.title: Crew Module
crew-module.description: Pressurized capsule designed for three Vyomnauts with indigenous ECLSS, touchscreen avionics, and deceleration systems.

abort-safety.title: Abort & Safety
abort-safety.description: Demo missions (TV-D1, D2) validate quick escape motors and recovery in the Bay of Bengal with the Indian Navy.

mission-timeline.title: Mission Timeline
mission-timeline.description: Uncrewed flights in 2025–26 will precede the first crewed LEO mission, leveraging LVM3 and HAL-built crew modules.

chandrayaan-programme.title: Chandrayaan Programme
chandrayaan-programme.description: Chandrayaan-1 confirmed lunar water molecules. Chandrayaan-2 orbiter continues polar science. Chandrayaan-3 achieved a south-pole landing with Vikram + Pragyan in 2023.

mangalyaan-mars-orbiter-mission.title: Mangalyaan (Mars Orbiter Mission)
mangalyaan-mars-orbiter-mission.description: Launched in 2013 via PSLV, became the first Asian Mars mission to reach orbit on its maiden attempt, showcasing frugal engineering.

indian-space-highlights.title: Indian Space Highlights
indian-space-highlights.description: From Aryabhata (1975) to Aditya-L1 (2023), ISRO steadily strengthened indigenous launchers, spacecraft platforms, and deep-space communications.

Navigation.name: Navigation
Navigation.context: NavIC constellation provides complete regional navigation coverage for India and surrounding areas.

EarthObservation.name: Earth Observation
EarthObservation.context: Cartosat, Resourcesat, Oceansat, and RISAT fleets cover most civilian and defense Earth observation needs.

DisasterManagement.name: Disaster Management
DisasterManagement.context: INSAT weather satellites, RISAT radar, and Cartosat optical satellites provide comprehensive disaster monitoring.

Weather.name: Weather
Weather.context: INSAT-3D and INSAT-3DR provide primary weather monitoring, with some reliance on international data for global models.

insights.0: Navigation signals are entirely powered by NavIC constellation, enabling complete independence from foreign GNSS systems.
insights.1: Earth observation remains a core strength with Cartosat, Resourcesat, Oceansat, and RISAT series covering diverse applications.
insights.2: Disaster management leverages INSAT weather satellites and RISAT radar imaging for comprehensive monitoring and response.
insights.3: Weather services primarily use INSAT-3D and INSAT-3DR, with some international data integration for global weather models.

cyclones.title: Cyclones
cyclones.summary: Seasonal low-pressure systems from the Bay of Bengal demand fast imaging and ocean wind mapping.

INSAT-3D.role: Rapid-scan infrared images and sounding for IMD warnings.

INSAT-3DR.role: Advanced meteorological observation and weather forecasting for cyclone tracking.

Oceansat-3 (EOS-06).role: Scatterometer winds pinpoint cyclone intensity at sea.

response.0: INSAT rapid imaging delivers storm center fixes every 15 minutes.
response.1: Oceansat scatterometer vectors help fishermen advisories and landfall prediction.

floods.title: Floods
floods.summary: Monsoon-fed rivers and cloudbursts require day-night radar coverage for inundation mapping.

RISAT-2BR1.role: All-weather Synthetic Aperture Radar cuts through clouds for flood plain updates.

Cartosat-3.role: Very high-resolution optical shots guide relief logistics and breach monitoring.

response.0: RISAT provides district-level flood layers for NDMA dashboards.
response.1: Cartosat imagery supports bridge safety checks and evacuation routes.

forest-fires.title: Forest Fires
forest-fires.summary: Western Ghats and Himalayan forests rely on thermal monitoring and smoke plume tracking.

Scatsat-1.role: Ocean wind monitoring also supports fire weather pattern analysis.

RISAT-2BR1.role: Radar imaging for all-weather fire detection and monitoring.

INSAT-3D.role: Night-time infrared brightens fire pixels for quick response.

response.0: Thermal anomaly feeds slip into Van Agni alerting system.
response.1: Geostationary coverage keeps fire control rooms updated every 15 minutes.

landslides.title: Landslides
landslides.summary: Fragile Himalayan slopes need precise elevation models and rainfall-linked triggers.

Cartosat-2F.role: Stereo pairs offer 1 m DEMs to detect slope instability.

GSAT-30.role: Emergency SATCOM backhauls data from remote hill sensors.

response.0: Cartosat DEM differencing flags slope creep near highways.
response.1: GSAT-30 ensures uninterrupted SDRF communication during fiber cuts.

drought.title: Drought
drought.summary: Arid belts of Vidarbha and Rayalaseema need soil moisture, vegetation, and groundwater cues.

Resourcesat-2A.role: Multi-spectral Earth observation for vegetation and soil moisture monitoring.

RISAT-2BR1.role: SAR-derived soil moisture indices for agriculture advisories.

response.0: Resourcesat-2A provides vegetation indices for drought assessment.
response.1: RISAT moisture maps feed the National Agricultural Drought Assessment System.
